content.title: English Teacher & Coach
content.copy: Move your life forward with better English. I am an award-winning teacher with a background in Linguistics and product design. I provide straightforward, high-level instruction for people who are serious about their progress.

eiken.student: I need help with my TOEFL score!
eiken.juan: No problem. Let's build a system.

travel.student: I'm traveling to London next month!

business.student: I have a big presentation in three months.

test_taker.title: The Test-Taker
test_taker.description: Preparing for Eiken, TOEFL, or TOEIC or another English test? I help you bridge the gap between knowing grammar and hitting your target score. I’ve helped students secure the scores they needed to move abroad and change their lives.

professional.title: The Professional
professional.description: High-stakes meetings or international presentations? I’ve taught at global companies like Fujitsu and Eizo. We refine your professional voice so you can lead with confidence in any corporate environment.

explorer.title: The Expat
explorer.description: Moving abroad or living in a new country? I know the isolation of the language barrier. I focus on practical communication and the cultural nuance you need to actually feel at home anywhere in the world.

next_gen.title: The Academic
next_gen.description: For University students and researchers. Drawing on my history tutoring graduate students and professors, I help you master the high-level English required for academic success and international research.

hobbyist.title: The Lifelong Learner
hobbyist.description: Learning for the sake of mastery? You don’t need English for a job, but you’re serious about the craft. I help you treat language as a project, focusing on the logic and systems that make fluency a rewarding intellectual pursuit.

framework.title: My Approach
framework.subtitle: How we'll go about meeting your goals.

step1.title: The Diagnostic Audit
step1.label: Point 01
step1.description: I'm not a fan of generic placement tests. I analyze your current speech and writing to find the specific bottlenecks holding you back from the next level.

step2.title: Language History
step2.label: Point 02
step2.description: I use my background in Linguistics and my teaching experience to identify your specific language needs for use cases like professional communication or standardized tests.

step3.title: High-Stakes Prep
step3.label: Point 03
step3.description: We then focus on what matters to you. Whether it's a TOEFL exam, a corporate presentation, or a visa interview, we build the exact tools you need to succeed.

step4.title: Cultural Nuance
step4.label: Point 04
step4.description: English is more than just words. I teach you the social unwritten rules and pragmatics I've learned from living and working in global environments.

step5.title: The Work Loop
step5.label: Point 05
step5.description: I provide direct, honest feedback. We iterate on your progress using a consistent pattern to build confidence in the language and fuel further growth.

testimonials.title: Student Success Stories
testimonials.subtitle: Real Results from My Past Students

hiroki.name: Satoshi M.
hiroki.story: Six months before moving to the U.S., I needed to improve my TOEIC score and prepare for daily life. Juan helped me with everything, including cultural norms that might surprise me in a rural area. I still see the impact of that experience today.

elena.name: Mina K.
elena.story: I needed a specific TOEFL score to study abroad. After several attempts on my own, I worked with Juan and passed on my first try after only three months. He helped make my goal a reality. The test wasn't easy, but it was great having someone who truly understood the challenge.

kenji.name: Dr. Park
kenji.story: During my sabbatical at UCSD, I was paired with Juan in the English in Action program. We met once a week for two years. It was great to have his help with English related to my work, but also just to have someone to chat with.

saki.name: Takumi O.
saki.story: I used to be very shy and worried about my grammar. Juan helped me realize that English is about communication and personality. I started to thoroughly enjoy speaking English because I was no longer putting so much pressure on myself to be perfect.

language_friends.title: Language Friends Series
language_friends.desc: A book series designed for natural language acquisition through repetition and instructional UX, such as color and visual association.
language_friends.details: Language Friends is a children's book series launched on Amazon and Rakuten Kobo. Drawing on my background in linguistics, these books focus on natural acquisition patterns rather than rote memorization. They help young learners and their parents engage with English through fun visuals and clear learning systems.

yt_challenge.title: Juan & Moe's Language Switch
yt_challenge.desc: Real-time language challenges and scripts in English and Japanese. Growing our language skills together.
yt_challenge.details: This YouTube channel documents a real-time language challenge with my wife. We document the struggle, the systems, and the breakthroughs of learning in a bilingual environment. It shows students how a high-agency approach to language works in daily life.

english_lab.title: The English Lab
english_lab.desc: A mix of English, humor, and simple storytelling in a distinct visual style.
english_lab.details: The English Lab features short videos showing how to use expressive English in everyday—and sometimes absurd—situations. My goal is to help you learn without it feeling like work.

portfolio.title: Product & UX Portfolio
portfolio.desc: I also work as a freelance UX/Product Designer. Here you'll find my professional portfolio.
portfolio.details: My work as a Product Designer. This portfolio showcases how I solve complex problems through structured, scalable digital solutions. I apply this same 'Systems First' mindset to how I teach English.

unsplash.title: Photography
unsplash.desc: A collection of my photography, captured primarily with a Fuji X-S20.
unsplash.details: I love the process of photography. There is something grounding about capturing life as it happens. It is how I reset my creative systems. Please feel free to check out my work.

medium.title: Medium Blog
medium.desc: Articles on UX, pedagogy, and design systems.
medium.details: I write about the intersection of design, language, and logic. My articles explore how we can use architectural thinking to improve our learning habits, workflows, and our ability to communicate complex ideas.

contact.title: Let’s talk about your English.
contact.subtitle: Tell me where you’re currently stuck and what you want to achieve.

placeholders.name: Your Name
placeholders.message: What are your goals or current challenges with English?

identity.bio: I live and work on the coast of Ishikawa. I use my background in UX design and linguistics to help professionals communicate clearly and confidently. No fluff—just practical tools to help you get your point across.

success.title: Got it!
success.subtitle: Thanks for sharing that. I'm going to look over your goals now and I'll get back to you personally within 24 hours.

footer.mission: Breaking down language systems to help professionals communicate with clarity and confidence. From UX design to high-agency coaching.

resume.title: Career Timeline
resume.tagline: A quick overview of my professional journey

irc.desc: Assisted in teaching English to Iraqi refugees, focusing on facilitating small-group activities.

ucsd.desc: Completed my degree with a focus on syntax and phonetics—core concepts that continue to shape my analytical approach to teaching.

eia.desc: Volunteered as a language partner for international students and professors to bridge communication gaps.

aeon.desc: Taught 300+ students; dispatched to global firms like Fujitsu and Eizo. Developed custom curricula and received the Teacher of Distinction award.

westgate.desc: Taught intensive English programs at Meiji University (Izumi Campus) in Tokyo.

boe.desc: Language assistant for local schools, focusing on curriculum delivery and student engagement.

cafe.desc: Managed daily business operations alongside my wife; responsible for digital presence and web development.

freelance.desc: I merge thoughtful product design with clean visual systems, building end-to-end digital products for international audiences.

friends.desc: Launched a digital-first children's book series designed for early-stage English exposure.

youtube.desc: Teaching English and documenting Japanese language challenges to demonstrate real-world learning logic.
